brand_dna.category: Family-focused wellness drink mixes - greens, protein, and clean energy powders that taste like juice.
brand_dna.core_belief: Healthy living should be easy, accessible, and actually delicious for every family.
proof_points: Created by a mom and a clinical/research lab scientist with 13 years experience
Zero sugar and no artificial dyes
brand_dna.positioning: Clean, science-backed daily nutrition that trades chalky, clinical supplements for juice-like flavors the whole family (kids included) will crave.

voice_pillars.name: Warm and Mom-to-Mom
voice_pillars.desc: Speaks peer-to-peer to busy moms with encouragement and real-life empathy, not clinical distance.
voice_pillars.quote: We're here to remind you that you are amazing.

voice_pillars.name: Effortlessly Simple
voice_pillars.desc: Frames the product as one easy habit that fits into chaotic real life with no extra steps.
voice_pillars.quote: No complicated systems, no extra steps. Just simple habits that work.

voice_pillars.name: Science-Backed Clean
voice_pillars.desc: Leans on real expertise and clean formulation to build trust without sounding like a lab report.
voice_pillars.quote: Clean, science-backed support for your daily routine.

voice_pillars.name: Crave-Worthy
voice_pillars.desc: Sells taste first, positioning greens and protein as something you actually look forward to.
voice_pillars.quote: Greens that actually taste like juice.

phrases: Greens that actually taste like juice
a greens drink you'll actually crave
Loved by moms. Approved by kids.
Protein you'll actually look forward to drinking

icp: Busy moms who run their households and want convenient, clean, great-tasting nutrition for themselves and picky kids without overhauling their whole routine.

personas.name: The Chaos-Managing Mom
personas.description: Juggling carpools, snacks, and mornings out the door - moved by portable, no-fuss habits that fit real life ('blend, pour and go').

personas.name: The Picky-Eater Parent
personas.description: Struggles to get quality nutrition into fussy kids and sensitive bellies - moved by juice-like flavors kids actually approve of.

personas.name: The Label-Reading Wellness Seeker
personas.description: Wants clean, third-party-tested, science-backed formulas and no artificial dyes - moved by transparency and scientist-founder credibility.

offers: Save 10% on all subscriptions + FREE shipping always
Bundles (e.g. Get Up and Go Bundle: $85.49, reg $94.99)
14-day refund/return window (unopened items only, request RA# via support@sipsclub.com)

allowed.claim: Supports healthy digestion, regularity and gut health
Zero sugar and no artificial dyes
9g whey isolate protein, 3g fiber and 20 vitamins per serving
100mg caffeine and 200mg L-theanine to help promote calm focus without the jitters or crash
allowed.source: Get Up and Go Bundle page

allowed.claim: Created by a mom and a clinical/research lab scientist with 13 years experience

banned.claim: Cures or treats constipation (or any digestive disease/condition)
banned.why: Disease claim - product is not intended to diagnose, treat, cure or prevent any disease; the founder's story of a daughter's severe constipation must not be framed as a cure.

banned.claim: Lowers or controls blood sugar / treats diabetes
banned.why: Disease claim - 'balances/supports healthy blood sugar' is the safe structure/function line; do not imply treatment of a blood sugar disease.

banned.claim: Boosts or cures immune deficiency / prevents illness
banned.why: Disease claim - immune-supporting is allowed, curing or preventing disease is not.

banned.claim: Clinically proven to improve digestion or energy
banned.why: No cited study provided - 'clinically proven' cannot be used without a referenced clinical trial.

banned.claim: Guaranteed results or a specific timeline (e.g. 'regular in 3 days')
banned.why: Banned - guaranteed outcomes and specific health-outcome timelines are not permitted for supplements.

banned.claim: Testimonials implying a disease benefit (e.g. curing severe constipation)
banned.why: Testimonials that imply a disease benefit are treated as disease claims under the compliance rules.
